citations.quoted_text:
Title: Advanced Energy Materials
Content: electrochemical properties in Li/S batteries, with beneficial effects due to the greater solvation
of oxygen atoms in the glyme structure, thereby solubilizing and dissociating the Li salts and
The investigation of liquid electrolyte solvents for RT-Na/S starts with ethers, including
DOL/DME, TEGDME, and tetraglyme.

Studies suggest that the ether-based electrolytes are
likely to contribute to low capacity.41,61, 72 Although the theoretical end product Na2S could be
detected, the capacity is only about 350-550 mA h g-1, indicating that the sodiation reaction is
incomplete, with production of an overall composition of Na2Sx (3 ≤ x ≤ 5).

The
charge/discharge plateaus occur at ~1.85 V, which is close to the overall expected cell

The observation of the kinetic sta-
bility of Li in a number of nonaqueous
electrolytes induced a rapid proliferation
of research in the field of Li batteries
beginning in the 1950s, and the commer-
cialization of primary Li batteries followed
quickly in the late 1960s and early 1970s.

the LixMn2O4 electrode in the electrolyte (Mn2+ is a solu-
ble species that is produced from Mn3+ by the dispropor-
tionation reaction to Mn2+ and Mn4+), as well as to an onset
of the Jahn±Teller effect in deeply discharged LixMn2O4
